preamble: The shift from the nasalized close-mid back rounded vowel [õ] to the close back rounded vowel [u] involves raising and denasalization. Similar to other nasal vowel shifts, it marks a simplification and upward movement in the vowel space.

examples.note: General pattern of nasal vowel raising and denasalization.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
Alkire, T., & Rosen, C. (2010). Romance Philology.
